description: Did you see that? He literally just trotted through that dusty flowerbed, and yet somehow his paws look like they've never touched a speck of dirt. It’s some sort of built-in wizardry dogs have. My own retriever can plunge into a damp park, do one enthusiastic, high-velocity vibration, and she is instantly dry, fluffy, and smelling faintly of cedar. Meanwhile, I just spilled half my iced matcha onto this canvas tote bag and it’s a structural disaster forever. It’s honestly unfair how dogs possess this effortless, self-polishing coat system. They spend five minutes grooming their front legs with such focused, methodical neatness, and suddenly they look ready for a stroll down the avenue. They put our entire multi-step laundry and shower routine to absolute shame with nothing more than a quick, joyful shake.
